meta.description: An identifiable single public-infrastructure event — a specific occurrence reported at a particular time and place that touches the condition, quality, or management of public infrastructure and urban services (roads, water supply, sewage, drainage, street lighting, waste management, urban planning). Examples: a wave of citizen complaints about water shortage in a colonia during a specific period, an announced urban-planning project, a public discussion or policy decision affecting waste management. For SPECIFIC physical works or single incidents (one pothole, one sinkhole, one pipe break, one repair job) use `public_works_event` instead — that schema is for the discrete works/incidents themselves; this schema is for the broader civic events around infrastructure (complaint waves, policy announcements, planning decisions) that are still anchored to a specific time and location.

example.description: Vecinos de varias colonias del norte de Queretaro denuncian falta de agua recurrente y exigen mejoras en la red de distribucion durante la temporada de calor 2025.
example.context: La problematica se agrava en temporada de calor. La Comision Estatal de Aguas reconocio el problema y promete medidas en el segundo semestre.
example.affected_area: colonias del norte de la ciudad de Queretaro
citizen_complaints: Falta de agua recurrente en colonias del norte de la ciudad

estimated_cost.mention: 200 millones de pesos para rehabilitacion de la red

example.impact_description: Miles de hogares sin servicio durante varias horas al dia.

event_type.description: Type of public-infrastructure event from the catalogue. Choose the single most specific category that matches.

status.description: Current status of the event as reported.

name.description: Name of the event or initiative if it has one (e.g. 'Plan de Rehabilitacion Hidrica 2025'). Often null for spontaneous complaint waves.

description.description: Brief free-text description of the event — what was reported, who reported it, what is being demanded or announced.

context.description: Broader context: historical issues, policy background, related projects or investments.

relevance.description: Relevance within the article: 1 = main topic, 2 = secondary, 3 = implicit.

date_range.description: Date or date range when the event occurred or the discussion is anchored. Extract the start datetime and, if present, the end datetime.

location.description: Location of the public-infrastructure event. Should be as specific as the article allows.

responsible_authority.description: Government body or agency mentioned as responsible (e.g. 'Direccion de Obras Publicas', 'CONAGUA', 'Comision Estatal de Aguas').

affected_area.description: Description of the affected area — colonias, zones, stretches, or districts impacted by the event.

citizen_complaints.description: Citizen complaints or demands regarding infrastructure raised during this event, if mentioned. Each complaint as a separate item.

estimated_cost.description: Budget, investment, or cost figures mentioned for related projects, with original mention, numeric bounds, and currency.

impact_description.description: Description of the impact on services, traffic, or citizens.
